one_line: Haridasa saint-poet and systematizer of early Carnatic music in 16th‑century Karnataka.

appearance.age_look: mid-50s, lean and wiry with the calm presence of an elder musician-saint
appearance.face: Oval face with medium-brown South Indian complexion, thoughtful almond-shaped eyes, gently arched brows, a straight nose, trimmed grey-black moustache and short beard along the jawline, a vertical white Madhva tilaka with a central red streak on his forehead, and a serene half-smile that looks like he is always on the verge of singing.
appearance.hair: Short, neatly kept wavy hair receding slightly at the temples, streaked with grey, tied back into a small low knot at the nape, with a plain dark maroon cotton headband wrapped once around the forehead like a thin turban band.
appearance.costume: He wears a traditional Karnataka Vaishnava pilgrim’s outfit: a full-sleeved, knee-length off‑white cotton angavastra-style kurta with a simple round neck and side slits; over it a soft, handwoven saffron-yellow cotton uttariya draped from the left shoulder across the torso and around the back; a second narrower uttariya of deep maroon cotton is looped diagonally like a sash, knotted at his right hip with its end hanging to mid-thigh; his lower garment is a unpleated white cotton dhoti with a narrow mustard border, wrapped securely and reaching the ankles; he has simple dark brown leather paduka-style sandals; accessories are minimal and period-appropriate—a thin tulasi bead mala around his neck, one slightly thicker rudraksha-tulasi mixed mala resting lower on his chest, and a small cloth waist-pouch tied under the maroon sash; the textiles are plain-weave cotton with only modest woven borders, no heavy embroidery or metallic work, reflecting a renunciant yet dignified look.
appearance.signature: A tan-brown jackwood tambura almost as tall as he is, with a rounded gourd resonator, long plain neck, four visible tuning pegs, minimal dark wood inlay near the bridge, and a simple cotton strap so he can carry it slung over his right shoulder.

ref_desc: Purandara Dasa is a lean, medium-brown, middle-aged Haridasa with short grey-streaked hair in a small knot, Madhva tilaka, off-white kurta, white dhoti, saffron and maroon cotton drapes, tulasi malas, paduka sandals, and a tall tan-brown jackwood tambura.
sheet_prompt: Draw a full-body anime-style character model sheet of Purandara Dasa as a 16th‑century Haridasa musician-saint from Karnataka: a lean, medium-brown man in his mid-50s with an oval face, thoughtful almond eyes, short grey-streaked wavy hair tied into a small low knot, trimmed grey-black moustache and jawline beard, and a vertical white Madhva tilaka with a red streak on his forehead, wearing an off-white, knee-length, full-sleeved cotton kurta, a simple white ankle-length dhoti with a narrow mustard border, a soft saffron-yellow cotton shawl draped from the left shoulder across the torso, and a narrower deep maroon cotton sash diagonally across his chest knotted at the right hip, plus simple dark brown paduka-style sandals and two bead malas (one thin tulasi, one slightly thicker mixed) around his neck, with a small cloth waist-pouch at his side; he holds a tall tan-brown jackwood tambura with a rounded gourd body, long plain neck and four pegs, sometimes resting it beside him or slung over his right shoulder by a simple cotton strap; show front, three-quarter, and back views to clearly display the fixed hairstyle, tilaka, layered saffron and maroon drapes, dhoti, sandals, malas, waist-pouch, and tambura, all in a grounded historical style but with clean shonen-anime linework and the dominant colours of saffron yellow, off‑white, deep maroon, medium brown skin, and tan wood.
